task.title: Add dirty-primary baseline mode to task preparation.

context.problem_statement: agent-task-prepare blocks dirty primary checkouts by default, but safe isolated work can be blocked when unrelated primary-checkout dirt already exists. Add an explicit baseline mode that records current dirty state, allows isolated task work only by opt-in, and later proves whether the primary checkout changed after that baseline.
background: AGW-085 and AGW-086 added preflight/doctor and actionable dirty blockers.
AGW-088 added original-checkout baseline comparison for automation handoff.
AGW-092 added compact context bundles that surface dirty state and omissions.
The user reported on 2026-06-15 that automations and task starts fail when agents see unrelated dirty work in the target repo.
non_goals: Do not weaken the default clean-primary safety gate.
Do not silently ignore dirty tracked or untracked work.
Do not mutate, stash, revert, or clean user files.
Do not make baseline mode the default for write-capable task preparation.

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep repo-contract-kit as the install-layer guardrail provider for target repos, with explicit opt-in recovery paths that preserve user work and produce receipts.

area_contracts.purpose: Prepare isolated task worktrees and in-flight metadata for write-capable agents.
Verify handoff readiness for prepared task worktrees.
Installed target-repo Make surface for task preparation flags.
Operator-facing installed workflow and rollout guidance.

stop_conditions: Stop if baseline mode would allow task preparation without an explicit operator flag.
Stop if baseline metadata cannot prove whether the primary checkout changed after task preparation.
Stop if finalize/readiness/handoff logic cannot block or warn on primary drift after the baseline.

protected_files: User target repos outside the kit checkout.
Unrelated task lifecycle, closeout, and automation-handoff semantics.
expected_outputs: Explicit baseline flag for agent-task-prepare and installed Make target.
Dirty-state baseline with tracked/untracked entries, counts, HEAD, and deterministic state hash in metadata and receipt scaffolds.
Readiness/finalize or handoff guard that compares current primary state to the stored baseline and blocks on drift.
JSON/text output explaining the baseline and safe next command.
Docs, tests, version, changelog, and source backlog closeout.

acceptance_criteria.description: Default agent-task-prepare still blocks dirty primary checkouts.
A new explicit baseline mode allows preparation from a dirty primary checkout while recording the baseline in task metadata and receipt scaffolds.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Prepare tests inspect metadata and generated artifacts.

acceptance_criteria.description: The baseline includes enough deterministic evidence to compare later primary state.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Tests assert entries, counts, HEAD, changed files, and state hash.

acceptance_criteria.description: Readiness/finalize or handoff checks block or report when the primary checkout changed after the baseline.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Ready/finalize tests mutate primary after prepare and assert blocker output.

acceptance_criteria.description: Docs explain when baseline mode is appropriate and that it does not clean or bless unrelated dirt.

commands.command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest tests.test_agent_task_prepare tests.test_agent_task_ready tests.test_agent_task_finalize

evidence_to_capture: focused and full kit test output

docs_impact.notes: This changes installed task preparation, readiness, and dirty-state behavior.

known_risks: Baseline mode could be misread as permission to ignore unrelated user work.
Hashing untracked file contents must remain deterministic and local-only.
Drift checks must compare primary checkout state, not task worktree state.
stop_conditions: The design requires force-cleaning, stashing, or modifying user dirty files.
The guard cannot distinguish matching pre-existing dirt from new primary checkout drift.
The change would make dirty baseline mode implicit or automatic.

approval.notes: User asked to implement all remaining workflow-stack backlog items serially with one task agent per backlog item.

overlap_warnings: Use exactly one task worker for this backlog item. The worker owns kit implementation files only; the parent owns source task-packet and backlog closeout.

handoff.owner: one kit worker for implementation; parent for integration and closeout
